title: Cursor adds /automate for plain-English coding automations
friendlyTitle: Cursor can now help set up repeat coding tasks
summary: Cursor released improvements to Automations, including a /automate skill that lets users describe a task in natural language while Cursor configures the triggers, instructions, and tools. The update also adds Slack emoji triggers, more GitHub triggers, and computer-use support for cloud agents.
friendlySummary: You can describe a coding task in simple words, and Cursor helps create an automation for it. It can also start tasks from Slack reactions or GitHub events.
whyItMatters: This moves coding agents closer to repeatable operational workflows instead of one-off chat sessions. Teams can turn recurring bug triage, PR review follow-up, and workflow-failure checks into always-on assistants with less manual setup.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This can save developers time on repeated work like checking failed builds or responding to review comments. It makes AI feel more like a helpful teammate than a chat box.
